summary:
Thrombosis prophylaxis should not be implemented universally but instead guided by individualized risk assessment, targeting moderate-to-high-risk patients where benefits outweigh potential bleeding hazards. Evidence shows significant reductions in venous thromboembolism (up to 65%) with safe, effective agents like apixaban or rivaroxaban when used appropriately, supporting targeted而非 broad intervention.  

Final answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$
